name: Clerk API
what_it_does: Developer-first authentication and user management platform with prebuilt UI components, a REST API, and an MCP server for managing users, sessions, organizations, and auth flows in modern web apps.
use_cases: Drop-in auth for Next.js, React, and other modern frameworks with zero-config UI components
Managing users, invitations, and organization memberships via API in agent workflows
Implementing SSO, MFA, and social login (Google, GitHub, etc.) without building from scratch
Issuing and validating JWTs with custom claims for downstream service auth
Programmatic user provisioning and deprovisioning in SaaS onboarding/offboarding flows
not_for: Enterprise B2B SSO with SAML and SCIM directory sync at scale (use WorkOS for that)
Applications requiring on-premises or self-hosted auth infrastructure
Non-JavaScript ecosystems where prebuilt UI components offer no advantage
best_when: Developer teams building modern web apps (especially Next.js) want a best-in-class auth experience with minimal setup, prebuilt UI, and strong agent/MCP integration.
avoid_when: Your product serves enterprise customers requiring SAML SSO, SCIM directory sync, or strict data residency — WorkOS or Auth0 are more appropriate.

auth.notes: Backend API uses a Secret Key (sk_live_/sk_test_) for server-side requests. Frontend uses Publishable Key + session tokens. JWTs issued by Clerk are verifiable with the JWKS endpoint. Clerk's MCP server uses the Secret Key for admin operations. No granular per-key scoping — Secret Key is full-access.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free up to 10,000 monthly active users (MAU) with all core features including MFA, social login, and organizations
pricing.paid_tiers: Pro from $25/month (includes 10K MAU, then $0.02/MAU); Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Free tier is generous and includes social login, MFA, passkeys, and organizations. MAU pricing scales predictably. B2B SSO (SAML) and SCIM are Pro+. No credit card required for free tier.

performance.rate_limits: Backend API: ~100 requests/second per application; endpoint-specific limits apply

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Clerk returns structured JSON errors with a code, message, and long_message field — one of the better error schemas in the auth space. MCP server exposes clear tool descriptions. Validation errors are field-specific and actionable.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: User creation is not idempotent — creating a user with an existing email returns an error rather than upserting. Use the users.list filter by email_address for check-or-create patterns. Invitation creation is idempotent by email within an organization.
known_agent_gotchas: Secret Key (sk_live_) has full admin access — no scoping possible; treat as high-privilege credential in agent config
Backend API and Frontend API are different surfaces — agents always use the Backend API with the Secret Key
User metadata has two tiers: public (visible to frontend) and private (backend only) — easy to leak sensitive data to the wrong tier
MCP server exposes admin-level user management — appropriate for backend agents, not user-facing agents
Session tokens are short-lived (1 hour) and frontend-only — agents use Secret Key, not session tokens
JWKS endpoint public keys rotate — agents verifying JWTs must implement JWKS refresh
Webhooks are signed with svix — must verify signature on every payload; unsigned webhook handlers are a security risk
Organization features require Pro plan — build org workflows only after confirming plan level
